name: AssigneeLock
description:
Models gastown's per-assignee sling lock (sling.go:912 tryAcquireSlingAssigneeLock). Three-client mutex for contending Slings. Each client owns its own ACQUIRE / GRANTED / RELEASE port pair, so the port-of-arrival carries the holder's identity -- no payload routing needed. State Free / HeldBy1 / HeldBy2 / HeldBy3 tracks the holder. RELEASE_N is only enabled when the lock is in HeldByN.

v0.2: extended from 2 clients to 3 clients to support the multi-bead, multi-formula composition (defense-doc P2.5). The lock is keyed by assignee, not by formula -- all slings on the same assignee share this lock regardless of formula. Three clients = three concurrent contenders.

Modeled after kinner-stdlib's Mutex (experiments/concurrency/lib/Mutex.kinner.json) -- this is the same shape with gastown-specific naming and one extra client. The defense-doc P1.2 demonstrates that even with the lock serializing contenders, the singleton-by-formula bug still fires; the lock is not the answer.

actors.name: AssigneeLock
actors.owner: Per-assignee sling lock. Free / HeldBy1 / HeldBy2 / HeldBy3.
